authoritative_sources.title: Occupancy Requirements of Subsidized Multifamily Housing Programs (4350.3)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD is the federal agency for subsidized multifamily housing programs; Handbook 4350.3 is HUD's operating handbook for tenant selection, leases, resident notices, EIV, termination, and file evidence that frame resident relations in HUD-assisted multifamily housing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Resident identity and occupancy grounding, lease-aware communication, resident notice review, tenant-file evidence, and assisted-housing issue routing
used_for: tenant file and notice evidence expectations
boundary handling for eligibility, rent, subsidy, termination, and legal-sensitive issues

authoritative_sources.title: Management Agent Handbook 4381.5 REV-2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's management-agent handbook directly describes owner, management agent, resident, resident association, and HUD relationships and identifies communication, management access, resident input, and prompt problem resolution as management-agent expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Resident communication intake, resident association handling, management access, resident input, and resident problem resolution

authoritative_sources.title: Resident Rights and Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's resident-facing rights and responsibilities material is authoritative public HUD guidance for resident expectations, decent safe and sanitary housing language, complaint posture, and resident obligations in HUD-assisted contexts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Resident-facing communication, complaint intake, resident responsibilities, and communication tone constraints
used_for: tenant responsibility and management response boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 966 - Public Housing Lease and Grievance Procedure
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 966 is HUD's regulation for public housing lease and grievance procedures, making it authoritative for resident relations cases that implicate PHA lease disputes, grievance triggers, notices, and process-sensitive handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public housing lease issue triage, grievance-sensitive communication, deadline capture, and legal or grievance owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 245 - Tenant Participation in Multifamily Housing Projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 245 governs tenant participation and tenant organizations in covered multifamily housing projects, directly supporting resident association and tenant input communication workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Resident association communication, tenant organization meeting and input handling, and owner or management response tracking
used_for: refusal when generic customer service would ignore resident input rights

authoritative_sources.title: Fair Housing
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD FHEO enforces federal fair housing law; HUD fair housing guidance is authoritative for protected-class, discrimination, harassment, retaliation, and accommodation-sensitive escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fair housing indicator detection, protected information handling, accommodation-sensitive routing, and resident complaint escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 Subpart L - Protection for Victims of Domestic Violence, Dating Violence, Sexual Assault, or Stalking
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's VAWA rule controls covered housing provider notice, documentation, confidentiality, and emergency transfer obligations; it is authoritative for resident relations cases involving domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking facts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: VAWA-sensitive intake, confidentiality control, emergency transfer routing, and non-disclosure escalation
used_for: refusal of eligibility or transfer approval authority

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 Subpart B - Disclosure and Verification of Social Security Numbers; Procedures for Obtaining Income Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 5 Subpart B governs SSN, income information, consent, Privacy Act, and EIV handling in covered HUD programs, making it authoritative for resident relations treatment of identity, income, and resident file data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Resident identity verification support, privacy-sensitive evidence handling, consent-aware routing, and restricted data minimization

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 Subpart G - Physical Inspection of Real Estate
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 5 Subpart G establishes HUD physical inspection requirements under NSPIRE and is authoritative for inspection evidence, condition deficiencies, and resident-facing safety or completion status boundaries in covered housing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Maintenance status communication, inspection evidence review, safety-sensitive escalation, and completion-status refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Service Coordination Partnership Toolkit: Housing Providers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACL is a federal agency serving older adults and people with disabilities; its housing provider toolkit clarifies when resident concerns become service coordination or supportive service partnership workflows rather than property management communication alone.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Resident services referral, service coordination handoff, supportive service boundary detection, and adjacent-owner routing

workflow_stages.name: Resident request intake and safety screening
workflow_stages.description: Capture resident identity, property, unit, request channel, incident date, request type, emergency indicator, program context, language access needs, communication preference, privacy sensitivity, and requested outcome before producing resident-facing communication.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-of-truth and policy grounding
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve lease, house rules, resident policy, complaint and grievance procedures, work-order status, inspection evidence, resident notices, tenant participation records, accommodation or VAWA artifacts, and tenant authority matrix from named systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Request classification and evidence triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify the contact as informational, complaint-driven, service-status follow-up, repeat failure, policy exception, resident association matter, grievance-sensitive, fair housing or accommodation-sensitive, VAWA-sensitive, safety-sensitive, owner-transfer sensitive, or blocked on prerequisites.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported response or resolution packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed resident communication, service disposition, unresolved item list, next-step owner, follow-up date, and traceability back to property management, CRM, work-order, inspection, policy, complaint, or resident file records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and resident-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate policy exceptions, unsupported workarounds, recurring repairs, safety-sensitive conditions, conflicting policies, missing evidence, resident association disputes, grievance triggers, privacy constraints, and service recovery issues with a resident-safe evidence packet.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Adjacent-owner handoff and refusal
workflow_stages.description: Return legal, fair housing, accommodation, VAWA, grievance, eviction, rent, subsidy, eligibility, maintenance, inspection, safety, service coordination, finance, and executive authority to the correct owner while preserving evidence and communication history.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Resident profile, household record, lease, lease addenda, house rules, tenant selection plan excerpt, resident notices, communication preferences, consent and release records, and language-access records
Property, building, unit, program stack, occupancy status, subsidy context, resident association, and management-agent records

Work orders, maintenance tickets, emergency maintenance logs, inspection reports, NSPIRE or local code references, photos, vendor notes, completion notes, punch lists, and resident confirmation records
CRM cases, resident portal messages, phone logs, complaint logs, resident impact notes, service recovery requests, follow-up commitments, and communication templates
Grievance, appeal, lease enforcement, tenant participation, resident association meeting, house-rule change, and management response records

Reasonable accommodation intake records, fair housing escalation indicators, VAWA notices and emergency transfer records, confidentiality flags, service coordinator referrals, and privacy-sensitive resident file references
systems_of_record: Affordable housing compliance software or HUD/PHA systems where applicable
Complaint, grievance, and appeal tracking system
Reasonable accommodation and fair housing case tracker
VAWA emergency transfer and confidentiality-controlled records
Resident services or service coordination referral system

allowed: Classify resident contacts as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or escalation-required.
Request minimum facts and source records required for safe resident-facing communication.
Summarize documented status from lease, complaint, work-order, inspection, resident notice, and policy records.
Prepare resident-safe communication, resolution notes, request-resolution summaries, and escalation packages.
Apply documented tenant communication, complaint, service-level, and escalation rules without improvising exceptions.
Route to adjacent owners when legal, fair housing, VAWA, grievance, maintenance, inspection, rent, subsidy, or approval authority is required.
escalate: Repeat work-order or resident-impact failure with no stable owner or root-cause record.
Safety, habitability, accessibility, inspection, emergency maintenance, or environmental indicators.
Reasonable accommodation, assistance animal, protected-class, harassment, retaliation, or fair housing indicators.
VAWA-sensitive disclosure, confidentiality, or emergency transfer request.
Public housing grievance, appeal, lease enforcement, eviction, or tenant rights question.
Rent, subsidy, eligibility, transfer, waitlist, fee waiver, compensation, or policy exception request.
Conflicting lease, house-rule, local law, tenant policy, agency rule, or system record.
refuse: Approve or deny accommodations, VAWA protections, emergency transfers, grievances, evictions, eligibility, rent or subsidy corrections, transfers, fee waivers, compensation, or policy overrides.
Declare a unit safe, habitable, inspection-passed, accessible, repaired, or ready for occupancy without authorized maintenance or inspection evidence.
Disclose resident personal, disability, income, household, VAWA, complaint, or accommodation information outside approved channels.
Represent unsupported workarounds, backdated completion, or generic resident service language as authoritative.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic customer service response ignores housing records
domain_failure_modes.impact: Resident-facing answer may omit lease terms, grievance rights, work-order evidence, tenant participation, fair housing, privacy, or safety constraints.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require property, unit, resident, policy, source-system, and artifact grounding before resolution.

domain_failure_modes.impact: Resident may be told a repair, inspection, or hazard is resolved without maintenance, inspection, or authorized safety evidence.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Block completion language and escalate to maintenance, inspection, safety, or property management owners.

domain_failure_modes.impact: The lane could unlawfully approve, deny, discourage, or mishandle disability or protected-class information.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Use minimum necessary handling and route to fair housing or accommodation owner.

domain_failure_modes.impact: Sensitive survivor information could be exposed or an emergency transfer request mishandled.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Apply confidentiality flags, approved systems, restricted detail, and VAWA owner routing.

domain_failure_modes.impact: Resident rights, lease enforcement, appeal, or eviction questions could be answered without authorized legal or grievance process authority.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Capture process facts and route to grievance or legal owner.

domain_failure_modes.impact: Market-rate, LIHTC, HUD-assisted, and PHA housing contexts could receive the same script despite different rules and records.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Record program stack, jurisdiction, lease, local policy, source precedence, and assumptions before adaptation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local landlord-tenant notice, habitability, repair, entry, fee, transfer, and grievance-like procedures vary by jurisdiction and lease.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction, lease, house rules, local policy, and legal owner precedence before resident-facing commitments.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Resident relations workflows differ across market-rate, LIHTC, HOME, project-based Section 8, public housing, and mixed-finance properties.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require property program stack, tenant policy, authority matrix, and systems-of-record retrieval before adapting scripts or dispositions.

research_to_pack_constraints: The pack must mention housing-specific resident records, including lease, unit, work-order, inspection, grievance, resident association, fair housing, accommodation, VAWA, and privacy artifacts.
The pack must refuse legal, fair housing, VAWA, accommodation, grievance, rent, subsidy, maintenance, inspection, safety, and policy override authority.

Evaluation scenarios must prove missing evidence, repeat failure, policy exception, resident association terminology, VAWA confidentiality, accommodation boundary, grievance routing, conflicting source handling, and tenant adaptation.
